prompt:
A 64 GB microSD card lying on a wooden desk next to a USB microSD card reader and a Raspberry Pi-style single-board computer in the background. The card has a simple black body with a small colored label area that uses abstract shapes instead of readable branding, and “64 GB” is implied by iconography rather than text. A faint glow on the contacts and the nearby board hints that this card is used to flash an operating system before cloning to an SSD.

Near-future solarpunk electronics aesthetic with warm lighting, neat cable management, and a small plant or solar gadget on the edge of the frame. Photorealistic 512×512 rendering, no brand logos or legible text anywhere on the card or reader. The composition should clearly emphasize the microSD card as a standalone inventory item with a sense of capacity and purpose.
